steps.title: Spot the key tension
steps.content:
The word “however” signals a direct contrast: Levine & Zervos found no enduring investment increases from liberalization (studying 1976-1993), while Henry found significant increases in the three years immediately after liberalization.

The phrase “taken together” requires reconciling these seemingly opposed findings.

steps.title: Reconcile both findings
steps.content: D directly resolves the tension: short-term investment gains (as Henry found) that don't endure (as Levine & Zervos found). The other choices each fail to capture this dual pattern. This confirms D is correct.
